lecture_context.lecture_title: What We See and What We Value: AI with the Human Perspective

unit.name: Bias, fairness, and privacy in visual AI
unit.description: Uses visual illusions, face recognition, and privacy-preserving sensing to argue that AI must account for human bias and respect human values.
unit.summary: Using the checkerboard luminance illusion, Fei-Fei Li argues that bias is built into human vision through evolutionary priors, social experience, and data exposure, which makes fairness a central issue for AI systems trained from human-generated data [ts=1863s] [ts=1919s]. She gives face recognition as a concrete example where systems historically performed differently across skin color and gender, with potential downstream harms in self-driving and medical use cases, while noting that by 2025 the field is paying much more attention to the problem [ts=1952s] [ts=1990s]. The unit then turns to privacy-preserving visual AI, contrasting blunt approaches like blurring and masking with a hardware-software lens that removes identity cues while preserving enough motion information for action recognition [ts=2034s] [ts=2148s].

key_points.text: Human visual perception contains biases shaped by evolution, social experience, and data exposure.
Bias becomes harmful when it creates unfairness for groups or communities.
Face recognition systems historically performed unevenly across skin color and gender.
Privacy in visual AI can be pursued with blurring, masking, dimensionality reduction, federated learning, or encryption.
A custom lens-plus-software approach can hide identity while retaining activity information for recognition.

kp_catalog.name: Bias and fairness in visual AI
kp_catalog.description: AI systems can inherit and amplify human or data bias, making fairness analysis essential for deployment.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because bias and fairness in visual AI formalizes how aI systems can inherit and amplify human or data bias, making fairness analysis essential for deployment, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Privacy-preserving visual AI
kp_catalog.description: Vision systems should preserve useful activity signals while withholding identity or other sensitive information.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because privacy-preserving visual AI clarifies vision systems should preserve useful activity signals while withholding identity or other sensitive information, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Human-centered AI
kp_catalog.description: The lecture frames AI progress through the lens of human perception, human impact, and human values rather than only algorithmic performance. Vision systems can save lives and create value, but also propagate bias and cause harm in high-stakes settings.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because human-centered AI formalizes how the lecture frames AI progress through the lens of human perception, human impact, and human values rather than only algorithmic performance, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context though roles split between capstone and gateway.
